archetype: Automated Offshore Wind Farm Wake Effect Mitigation and Turbine Control System
description: This system uses real-time weather and turbine performance data to optimize turbine placement and yaw control to minimize wake effects, increasing overall wind farm efficiency by up to 15%. It is used by wind farm operators and turbine manufacturers to improve energy production and reduce maintenance costs. The system must account for complex fluid dynamics, turbine interactions, and environmental factors.
rules: MUST adhere to IEC 61400-1 wind turbine design standards
MUST integrate with SCADA systems for real-time turbine data
MUST use mesoscale weather forecasting models for accurate wind predictions
MUST account for wake effects on downstream turbines
MUST comply with EU's Renewable Energy Directive (RED) for reporting and monitoring
anti_patterns: DO NOT use simplified wake models that neglect turbulence and rotor interactions
DO NOT neglect the impact of ocean currents and waves on turbine performance
DO NOT use fixed turbine yaw angles without considering real-time wind direction
key_technical_challenges: Accurate modeling of complex wake effects and turbine interactions
Real-time data processing and optimization of turbine control parameters
Integration with diverse SCADA systems and data formats

recommended_stack.language: Python - for its extensive libraries in scientific computing and data analysis
recommended_stack.framework: Django - for its robust web development capabilities and ease of integration with SCADA systems
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL - for its support of advanced geospatial data types and queries
recommended_stack.cache: Redis - for its high-performance caching and data processing
recommended_stack.message_broker: RabbitMQ - for its reliable and scalable message queuing
special_services: Copernicus Climate Data Store for weather forecasting
